refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined productivity target by optimizing equipment operation, task scheduling, and resource allocation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is broad and high-level; decomposing it into sub-FRs focused on key productivity drivers (equipment operation efficiency, task scheduling, resource allocation) allows targeted design and verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure productivity metrics (m³/h or ton/h) during representative operations; verify that each sub-FR contributes to meeting or exceeding the productivity target.
sub_frs: Optimize equipment operation to maximize throughput
Schedule tasks efficiently to minimize idle time
Allocate resources dynamically to meet productivity goals

candidate_dps: Advanced vehicle control algorithms for operation optimization
selected_dp.selected_dp: Advanced vehicle control algorithms for operation optimization

potential_sources: Interaction between vehicle control and task scheduling
Resource allocation dependencies on vehicle operation
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on vehicle operation optimization, which can be modularized with clear interfaces to scheduling and resource allocation, minimizing coupling.

assumptions_risks: Assumes vehicle control algorithms can be effectively isolated from scheduling and resource allocation logic
Risk that changes in scheduling policies may require vehicle control adjustments
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicit interface definitions between vehicle control and scheduling modules to manage coupling.

im_boundary.name: Vehicle
im_boundary.scope: Physical and software components responsible for vehicle operation and control

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Vehicle control module sends operational data to NGHE Solution for monitoring and analysis

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives task commands from NGHE Solution to adjust vehicle operation accordingly

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Communication channel for remote control and data exchange

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment of vehicle control logic; low diffusion as interfaces are well-defined
Contains higher-level coordination but less direct control over vehicle operation
Supports remote control but not primary vehicle control logic
Primarily focused on safety alerts, minimal containment of productivity control

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Vehicle module is the best containment boundary for the advanced vehicle control algorithms due to direct control over physical equipment and software integration.
containment_risks: Potential overlap with Teleoperation module if remote control algorithms are tightly coupled
Risk of interface complexity increasing if task scheduling commands are not well standardized
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Clarify interface contracts between Vehicle and Teleoperation modules to reduce diffusion risk.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The refined FRs are well aligned with the selected DP, focusing on vehicle operation optimization as a key productivity driver.

diffusion_risks: Possible diffusion of control logic between Vehicle and Teleoperation modules
Interface complexity with NGHE Solution coordination
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Vehicle module shows strong containment of the selected DP with well-defined external interfaces.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by monitoring telemetry data and tasking commands at the Vehicle module boundary.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of vehicle control DP to Vehicle IM with explicit interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Define and document interface contracts between Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.
Review tasking interface to ensure minimal coupling and clear command semantics.

decision.justification: The current level of functional decomposition, DP selection, and IM assignment meet the criteria for clarity, containment, and verification feasibility with manageable risks.
next_level_sub_frs: Optimize equipment operation to maximize throughput
Schedule tasks efficiently to minimize idle time
Allocate resources dynamically to meet productivity goals

revise.why: To clarify and strengthen interface contracts between Vehicle and Teleoperation modules to reduce diffusion risk.

assumptions: Vehicle control algorithms can be modularized within the Vehicle IM boundary.
Tasking commands from NGHE Solution are standardized and stable.
open_risks: Interface complexity between Vehicle and Teleoperation modules may increase diffusion.
Changes in scheduling or resource allocation may impact vehicle control logic.
evidence_needed: Interface specification documents between Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.
Telemetry and tasking data logs demonstrating effective control and monitoring.
